prompt:
Our CAD service checks geometry-kernel sessions out of a floating-license server; every session is expensive to open and we only pay for N concurrent seats. The hand-rolled pool we inherited loses seats: sessions get returned twice (so two workers share one kernel), leaked leases are invisible until the license server starts refusing us, and shutdown just abandons whatever was checked out. Rebuild it properly as a tiny generic library — single-threaded and fully deterministic, JDK only, three sibling .java files (no packages): Lease.java, LeasePool.java, PoolExhaustedException.java.

Lease.java — public interface Lease<R> extends AutoCloseable with: R get(); int id(); boolean active(); void discard(); and close() overridden to throw nothing. A lease is single-owner and dies exactly once: close() returns the resource to the pool, discard() is for broken resources — it retires the resource on the spot and frees the seat WITHOUT returning it. Whichever of close/discard happens first wins; any later close/discard on the same lease is a silent no-op (this is the double-return bug killer — the tests prove a double close cannot create a phantom idle entry). After the lease is dead, get() throws IllegalStateException with message exactly "lease N is closed" / "lease N was discarded" / "lease N was annulled by pool close" depending on how it died.

PoolExhaustedException.java — public class PoolExhaustedException extends RuntimeException.

LeasePool.java — public final class LeasePool<R> implements AutoCloseable:
- Constructor (int maxSize, Supplier<? extends R> factory, Consumer<? super R> retire). Validation as IllegalArgumentException with exact messages "maxSize must be >= 1", "factory must not be null", "retire must not be null".
- Lease<R> acquire(): reuse an idle resource if there is one — LIFO, most recently returned first — else create one lazily through the factory while idle+outstanding < maxSize, else throw PoolExhaustedException("pool exhausted: all N permits leased"). A factory that returns null → IllegalStateException("factory returned null"); a factory that throws → propagate untouched; either way the pool's counters and capacity are unaffected and NO lease id is consumed. Lease ids are 1, 2, 3, ... per successful acquire (fresh id even when the resource is reused).
- Accounting for leak hunting: idleCount(), outstandingCount(), createdCount() (factory successes ever), retiredCount() (retire calls ever), and List<Integer> outstandingIds() — ascending lease ids currently checked out, unmodifiable; this is our leak report.
- close(): idempotent, and it ANNULS rather than waits — we are single-threaded, nobody can return anything later. First close retires every idle resource (LIFO order, most recently returned first), then every outstanding lease's resource in ascending lease-id order; outstanding leases flip to the annulled state (active() false, get() throws the annulled message, their close()/discard() become no-ops that must NOT call retire again). After close: acquire() throws IllegalStateException("pool is closed"), idleCount()/outstandingCount() are 0, outstandingIds() is empty, and retiredCount() == createdCount(). A second close() retires nothing more.

TestMain.java is the acceptance contract — it pins the LIFO reuse, the exact messages, the retire ordering on close, the leak report, and the no-op rules, using try-with-resources on both the lease and the pool. Leave it alone. Done = `java TestMain.java` all PASS, exit 0.
